sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: By Hartley's theorem (based on CFSG), if a simple locally finite group does not contain a particular finite group as a section, then it is isomorphic to a group of Lie type over a locally finite field. [r]{a)} Is the same true for an infinite definably simple locally finite group? A group is said to be definably simple if it does not contain proper definable normal subgroups. [r]{b)} The same ques...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
